criteria.evidence: Constraints: Days until interview: 10; Rounds: recruiter screen (30m) → hiring-manager screen (45m...) → onsite (virtual): people-management case · cross-functional/conflict scenario · technical-direction/design discussion · values interview.
criteria.reason: Timeline (10 days) and the full round structure are captured verbatim from the JD and actively used to drive the day-by-day cadence.

criteria.evidence: **This loop is leadership-weighted, NOT a coding gauntlet.** Do not over-invest in algorithm/coding drills. Front-load behavioral stories, management philosophy, and the people/conflict cases.
criteria.reason: Sequencing front-loads the next round (HM screen: stories + philosophy on Days 1-2) and mirrors the JD's leadership-weighted onsite shape exactly. The headline round-awareness test is met.

criteria.evidence: Priority queue table with columns Action / Skill to run / Maps to, e.g. 'Close the performance-management gap... | build-curriculum | onsite people-management case'.
criteria.reason: Every queue item names a concrete skill and the specific round/gap it serves; gaps (perf mgmt, hiring) map cleanly to skills and rounds.

criteria.evidence: 'Done' looks like: Deliver all 5–7 stories cold, each in ≤90s, each landing on a concrete decision + a real number (18→6 min, ~40% incidents, 2 promoted to senior). State a coherent management philosophy in ≤60s... Name the formal-management gap in one honest sentence and immediately pivot.
criteria.reason: Concrete, testable readiness bars with timeboxes and named-framework requirements, not platitudes.

criteria.evidence: Do not over-invest in algorithm/coding drills... Action #3 'Close the performance-management gap', #4 'Close the hiring-as-hiring-manager gap'.
criteria.reason: No coding/LeetCode grind is prescribed; effort goes to the real leadership/people-management/hiring gaps. Technical-direction round is rightly given light weight given her over-qualification. No mis-prioritization.
